title: Article 126a.  Military magistrates
doc_html:
§126a. Article 26a. Military magistrates

            A. A military magistrate shall be a commissioned officer of the state military forces who:

            (1) Is a member of the bar of a federal court or a member of the bar of the highest court of a state; and

            (2) Is certified to be qualified, by reason of education, training, experience, and judicial temperament, for duty as a military magistrate by the state judge advocate.

            B. In accordance with the Federal Rules of Courts-Martial or regulations prescribed by the governor or adjutant general, in addition to duties when designated under Article 19 or 30a of this Code, a military magistrate may be assigned to perform other duties of a nonjudicial nature.

            Acts 2019, No. 373, §1.

§126a.  Article 26a.  Military magistrates
A.  A military magistrate shall be a commissioned officer of the state military forces
who:
(1)  Is a member of the bar of a federal court or a member of the bar of the highest
court of a state; and
(2)  Is certified to be qualified, by reason of education, training, experience, and
judicial temperament, for duty as a military magistrate by the state judge advocate.
B.  In accordance with the Federal Rules of Courts-Martial or regulations prescribed
by the governor or adjutant general, in addition to duties when designated under Article 19
or 30a of this Code, a military magistrate may be assigned to perform other duties of a
nonjudicial nature.
Acts 2019, No. 373, §1.
